extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by rec_a_from_conjunction (priority 50), which requires immunosuppressed AND asthma. rec_drug_b is set true only by rec_b_from_trigger (priority 50), which requires immunosuppressed. The remaining three rules set only rec_drug_c and are irrelevant. When immunosuppressed=true and asthma=true, both governing rules fire on their respective (distinct) outputs with no competing higher-priority rule to suppress either, so rec_drug_a=true and rec_drug_b=true simultaneously. This makes (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b) jointly true, violating the mutual-exclusion property. Witness: immunosuppressed=true, asthma=true, others false (their values do not matter; they only affect rec_drug_c). Verification of witness: rec_a_from_conjunction yields rec_drug_a=true; rec_b_from_trigger yields rec_drug_b=true; defaults do not apply since both outputs are set; property violated.
